title: CI/CD Pipeline Failed
scenario: The CI/CD pipeline calls a deploy script that was working fine yesterday, but today the deployment stage fails immediately. The deploy.sh script is still on the server but it refuses to run.
symptoms: Script file exists but cannot be executed
mission: Investigate why deploy.sh cannot run, fix the permissions, and execute it successfully.

hints.title: Investigate the error
hints.content: Try running `./deploy.sh` and observe the error. Then use `ls -l deploy.sh` to inspect the file's permissions.

hints.title: Understand the permission bits
hints.content: The output of `ls -l` shows permissions like `-rw-r--r--`. The `x` bit is needed for a file to be executable. Notice it is missing.

hints.title: The fix
hints.content: Run `chmod +x deploy.sh` to add the execute permission, then run `./deploy.sh` to confirm it works.
